Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 10:06 ET
Healthcare calls surge into earnings while tech diverges despite negative gamma regime
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Healthcare names dominate unusual call flow with UNH, HUM leading into earnings week positioning
  • 2. Tech shows stark vol-flow divergence with rich implied vol amid call-heavy activity in AAPL, MSFT
  • 3. Negative $9.7B SPY gamma amplifies all directional moves ahead of CPI Wednesday
NARRATIVE — negative gamma CPI setup

Healthcare calls are commanding unusual flow attention with UNH (+93% call vol, P/C 0.43) and HUM (+58%, P/C 0.50) leading the charge into earnings season. This sector rotation coincides with both names trading at steep discounts to sector vol — UNH showing -33% cheap vol versus sector ETF despite the call surge. The divergence suggests either mispriced protection or informed positioning ahead of quarterly results, with Millennium's mixed MSFT positioning ($2.49B Q4 add) indicating broader rotation themes at play. Tech presents the day's most compelling cross-signal tension with AAPL, MSFT showing rich vol/call heavy divergence flags. AAPL trades with 16-day backwardation and vol 145% rich to sector despite sustained call interest, while Millennium's $1.8B conviction build conflicts with current bearish technical scores (MSFT mixed, META score 23.2). The 0.23 implied correlation reading signals stock-specific regime favoring single-name vol over index hedging — exactly opposite to the index put-heavy 2.13 P/C ratio institutional players are deploying. The $9.7B negative SPY gamma environment amplifies Wednesday's CPI significance with dealers positioned to chase momentum in either direction. NVDA's massive 5,000-lot April 8 185C block at $0.04 and TSLA's 32,274-lot 340P position reflect the binary event setup, while Point72's persistent NVDA conviction build (#1 holding, 3-quarter accumulation) provides institutional backing for any upside acceleration through the $666.93 gamma flip.

Layer 1: Sentiment -8Layer 2: GEX -$9.7BLayer 4: Vol divergence flagsLayer 9: CPI Wednesday
